evals.prompt: Build a 21-day cold outbound cadence targeting VP of Engineering at Series B SaaS companies. Channels: email, phone, LinkedIn. Our product is a developer productivity platform.
evals.expected_output: A complete cadence architecture table with day-by-day steps across email, phone, and LinkedIn. Full content for each step including email copy with A/B subject lines, call scripts with voicemail, and LinkedIn messages. Should include personalization guidance and follow channel mixing best practices.

assertions.name: has_cadence_table
assertions.description: Includes a day-by-day cadence architecture table with step number, channel, and action

assertions.name: has_full_content
assertions.description: Writes actual email copy, call scripts, and LinkedIn messages — not just placeholders

assertions.name: has_ab_variants
assertions.description: Provides A/B subject line variants for at least the first email

assertions.name: follows_channel_mixing
assertions.description: Alternates channels rather than sending 3+ consecutive touches in the same channel

evals.prompt: My cold email cadence has a 35% open rate but only 2% reply rate. Help me optimize it.
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis of the gap between open rate and reply rate with specific optimization recommendations. Should cover email body improvements, CTA changes, personalization level, and an A/B testing plan to systematically improve reply rate.

assertions.name: diagnoses_gap
assertions.description: Identifies that 35% open rate with 2% reply rate indicates subject lines work but body/CTA needs improvement

assertions.name: has_specific_fixes
assertions.description: Provides specific, actionable changes to email body, CTA, or personalization

assertions.name: has_testing_plan
assertions.description: Includes an A/B testing plan to validate the recommended changes

evals.prompt: Design a trigger-based cadence for when a target account gets a new round of funding. We sell financial planning software to CFOs at mid-market companies.
evals.expected_output: A cadence specifically designed around the funding trigger event, with messaging that references the funding round and connects it to the need for financial planning software. Should leverage the timeliness of the trigger for urgency.

assertions.name: leverages_trigger
assertions.description: Messaging explicitly references the funding event and connects it to the prospect's likely new needs

assertions.name: has_cadence_structure
assertions.description: Includes a complete cadence table with timing and channels

assertions.name: persona_appropriate
assertions.description: Content is tailored for CFO-level audience at mid-market companies
